ManSection.Description:
Generates LaTeX tikz-code for a nice projective picture of the real 3-arrangement. If x,y,z are the coordinates, by default, this is the 2-dim affine arrangement obtained by intersecting A with the plane z=1.
The optional parameters are:
ps a scaling factor,
ip intersection points are drawn,
Hind labels for the hyperplanes are added,
disthv a vector giving the normal of the plane whith which to intersect A,
MarkHs a list of indices of hyperplanes of A, these are drawn in another color.
The example below will look as follows (only in pdf).
gap> A:=AGpql(2,2,3); <HyperplaneArrangement: 6 hyperplanes in 3-space> gap> Print(LaTeXDrawProjPicture(A)); \begin{tikzpicture}[scale=1.0] \draw (-2.827,2.827) -- (2.827,-2.827); % H_1 \draw (2.827,2.827) -- (-2.827,-2.827); % H_2 \draw (-1.,3.873) -- (-1.,-3.873); % H_3 \draw (1.,3.873) -- (1.,-3.873); % H_4 \draw (3.873,-1.) -- (-3.873,-1.); % H_5 \draw (3.873,1.) -- (-3.873,1.); % H_6 \end{tikzpicture} gap> Print(LaTeXDrawProjPicture(A,1/2,true,true,[1,1,1],[1,2])); \begin{tikzpicture}[scale=1.0] \draw[color=red] (-3.559,1.827) -- (1.827,-3.559); % H_1 \node at (1.975,-3.707) {\small $1$}; \draw[color=red] (2.827,2.827) -- (-2.827,-2.827); % H_2 \node at (-2.970,-2.970) {\small $2$}; \draw (3.361,2.169) -- (-3.995,0.197); % H_3 \node at (-4.198,0.143) {\small $3$}; \draw (-1.034,3.863) -- (1.034,-3.863); % H_4 \node at (1.087,-4.057) {\small $4$}; \draw (2.169,3.361) -- (0.197,-3.995); % H_5 \node at (0.143,-4.198) {\small $5$}; \draw (-3.863,1.034) -- (3.863,-1.034); % H_6 \node at (4.057,-1.087) {\small $6$};
\fill[red] (-0.865,-0.865) circle[radius=2pt]; % P[ 1, 2 ] \fill[red] (-2.366,0.634) circle[radius=2pt]; % P[ 1, 3, 6 ] \fill[red] (1.732,1.732) circle[radius=2pt]; % P[ 2, 3, 5 ] \fill[red] (0.634,-2.366) circle[radius=2pt]; % P[ 1, 4, 5 ] \fill[red] (0.0,0.0) circle[radius=2pt]; % P[ 2, 4, 6 ] \fill[red] (-0.317,1.183) circle[radius=2pt]; % P[ 3, 4 ] \fill[red] (1.183,-0.317) circle[radius=2pt]; % P[ 5, 6 ]
\end{tikzpicture}
\includegraphics{./LaTeX_Examples/DrawLatex3Arr_Example.pdf}
